title: Incrementation and decrementation doubles expression's side-effects
body:
AST for accessing value seems to get cloned for read and write access for pre/post incremenatation and decrementation operations. This results in any side-effects that could be present in the operand happening twice, first when reading the value and then when writing it.

Here's a minimal example for reproducing this behavior:
```systemverilog
int i = 0;

function int postincrement_i;
    return i++;
endfunction

module t;
    initial begin
        int arr [1:0] = {0, 0};
        arr[postincrement_i()]++;
        $display("Value: %d", i);
    end
endmodule
```

The expected output is
```
Value:           1
```
but instead we get
```
Value:           2
```

Note, I wrote a separate function `postincrement_i` because incrementing `i` directly in this context is yet another issue (which I'm already solving at the moment).

I'm using the latest version of Verilator under Linux (Gentoo):
```
Verilator 5.007 devel rev v5.006-101-g47a7e7584
```
